decisions.title: Why Two-Layer Injection: Names Upfront, Body on Demand
decisions.description: The system prompt includes only skill names and brief descriptions. The full skill body (detailed instructions and examples) is injected only when the agent decides to use a specific skill. This two-layer approach balances discoverability with context efficiency — the agent knows what skills exist without paying the token cost of loading them all.
decisions.alternatives: Loading all skill bodies upfront is simpler but wastes context on skills that may never be used. Loading nothing until the agent asks makes skills undiscoverable — the agent cannot request a skill it does not know exists. The two-layer design solves both problems.

zh.title: 为什么采用两层注入：名称前置，内容按需
zh.description: 系统提示中只包含技能名称和简短描述，完整的技能内容（详细指令和示例）只在 Agent 决定使用某个技能时才注入。这种两层方法在可发现性和上下文效率之间取得了平衡——Agent 知道有哪些技能可用，但不必为加载所有技能付出 token 代价。

decisions.title: Why Not Stuff Everything in the System Prompt
decisions.description: System prompts have practical limits. Beyond a certain length, the LLM's attention to specific instructions degrades. More importantly, every token in the system prompt is repeated on every API call, multiplying cost. Skills that are rarely used should not occupy permanent space in every request.
decisions.alternatives: We considered using a retrieval-augmented approach where skills are fetched from a vector store based on semantic similarity to the current query. While powerful, this adds infrastructure complexity. The name-based lookup is deterministic, fast, and sufficient for a bounded set of skills.

zh.title: 为什么不把所有内容都塞进系统提示
zh.description: 系统提示有实际的长度限制。超过一定长度后，LLM 对具体指令的关注度会下降。更重要的是，系统提示中的每个 token 在每次 API 调用时都会重复发送，成倍增加成本。很少使用的技能不应该在每次请求中占据固定空间。
